# ferriskey_identity_provider (Resource)

Manages a federation identity provider (broker) within a FerrisKey realm.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ferriskey_identity_provider" "google" {
  realm        = "acme"
  alias        = "google"
  provider_id  = "oidc"
  display_name = "Google"
  enabled      = true

  # Only the keys you set are managed; keys the server adds are ignored.
  config = {
    clientId     = var.google_client_id
    clientSecret = var.google_client_secret
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `alias` (String) Unique alias of the identity provider. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `provider_id` (String) Provider type, e.g. `oidc` or `saml`. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `realm` (String) Realm the identity provider belongs to. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `add_read_token_role_on_create` (Boolean) Whether to grant the read-token role on account creation.
- `config` (Map of String) Provider-specific configuration (e.g. `clientId`, `clientSecret`, `authorizationUrl`). Only the keys you set are managed; keys the server adds are ignored.
- `display_name` (String) Human-readable display name.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the identity provider is enabled. Defaults to `true`.
- `first_broker_login_flow_alias` (String) Authentication flow alias for first broker login.
- `link_only` (Boolean) Whether the IdP can only be used to link existing accounts.
- `post_broker_login_flow_alias` (String) Authentication flow alias for post broker login.
- `store_token` (Boolean) Whether tokens from the IdP are stored.
- `trust_email` (Boolean) Whether the IdP's email is trusted (skips verification).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Composite identifier `{realm}/{alias}`.
- `internal_id` (String) Server-assigned UUID.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Imported with the composite ID "{realm}/{alias}".
terraform import ferriskey_identity_provider.google acme/google
```
